ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general green tea beverage class; no specific 'honey green tea less sugar RTD' subclass exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP covers ready-to-drink green tea; no specific honey-flavoured less-sugar variant code exists. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for packaged honey green tea beverage; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve bottle of packaged honey green tea in Singapore is approximately 500 ml (500 g), providing about 115 kcal and 28.3 g of carbohydrates per bottle.

nutrient_source.title: Honey Green Tea (Less Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id D09020045 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All per_100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for packaged honey green tea less sugar RTD. GI estimated at 52 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sugar-sweetened tea beverages and sucrose-based drinks with similar sugar concentration (~5g/100ml). Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (52 × 28.3 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 14.7, rounded to 14.6. This is an analogous estimate and should be replaced with a direct measurement if one becomes available.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar content (5.43 g/100 ml) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently elevates blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 evidence, despite low sodium (6 mg/100 ml).
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this beverage and choose genuinely unsweetened green tea (0 g sugar/100 ml), as the 5.43 g/100 ml sugar load in this 'less sugar' RTD still qualifies it as a sugar-sweetened beverage that independently raises cardiovascular and blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 5.43 g sugar/100 ml and zero dietary fibre; liquid sugar raises postprandial blood glucose rapidly with no fibre-mediated attenuation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should not consume this beverage regularly; the 5.43 g/100 ml sugar with zero fibre and an estimated GI of 52 delivers approximately 14.6 glycemic load units per 500 ml bottle, and 'less sugar' labelling does not make it suitable for glycaemic management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages, even at reduced-sugar formulations, is associated with increased triglycerides, visceral adiposity, and cardiovascular event risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit or avoid this packaged honey green tea and opt for unsweetened green tea, which retains potential cardioprotective polyphenols without the added sugar burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar (5.43 g/100 ml) promotes pro-inflammatory mediators relevant to RA; no confirmed meaningful EGCG concentration in this packaged form offsets this effect.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should choose genuinely unsweetened packaged green tea to access any residual anti-inflammatory EGCG content without the pro-inflammatory added sugar present in this less-sugar formulation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Honey Green Tea Less Sugar (Packaged, Ready-to-Drink) is a commercially produced, ultra-processed bottled beverage widely available in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. It is made from water, green tea extract, honey, and added sugar, formulated to deliver a lighter sweetness profile than standard honey green tea RTDs. Per 100 ml, it provides 23 kcal, 5.66 g carbohydrates (of which 5.43 g is sugar), negligible protein (0 g by formulation), negligible fat (0 g by formulation), zero dietary fibre, and 6 mg sodium. Despite the 'less sugar' label, the product still qualifies as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under Singapore HPB and WHO definitions because it contains added caloric sweeteners. The beverage is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension due to its liquid sugar content, which is absorbed rapidly without fibre-mediated attenuation. For cardiovascular health, regular SSB consumption — even at reduced-sugar levels — is associated with elevated triglycerides and cardiovascular risk. Unsweetened green tea would be the preferred alternative for health-conscious consumers, as it retains polyphenols such as EGCG without the added sugar burden. The product is vegetarian, vegan, halal-friendly, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is commonly consumed as a breakfast or on-the-go beverage in Singapore.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D09020045, review_status reviewed, confidence 0.95). Exact match to an approved primary regional source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database, which slightly reduces overall confidence for the GI component only; all macronutrient data remain high confidence.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero by HPB source — this is a sugar-water-based beverage containing no protein-bearing ingredients by formulation. fat_g is confirmed zero by HPB source — no fat-bearing ingredients are present in this RTD tea formulation. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB; tea extract and honey in this dilute RTD form contribute no measurable fibre. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero consistent with zero total fat. calcium_mg and iron_mg are reported as 0.0 per HPB source. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid profiles for this specific packaged RTD honey green tea are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for D09020045. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for sugar-sweetened tea beverages at comparable sugar concentrations; a direct GI measurement for this specific product is not available in any approved source as of 2026-04-16. Glycemic load calculated for a 500 g (500 ml) standard bottle serving: GL = (52 × 28.3) / 100 = 14.7 (reported as 14.6 after rounding). 'Less sugar' labelling does not exempt this product from SSB classification under HPB or WHO criteria.
